result:
The error message now correctly shows `'x'` instead of `'?'`. Build is green.

**Root cause:** `LuaState::debug_find_local` in `crates/lua-vm/src/func.rs:785` was a Phase-B stub that always returned `None`, and `find_local` in `crates/lua-vm/src/debug.rs:520` set `name = None` for the Lua-function branch instead of calling `luaF_getlocalname`. As a result, `check_close_mth` always emitted `variable '?' got a non-closable value`, but the test on `locals.lua:504` expects the actual variable name (`'x'`).

**Fix:** Implemented `find_local` to call the existing `crate::func::get_local_name(&proto, n, pc)` (returning `Option<Vec<u8>>` rather than `&'a [u8]` since the proto borrow can't escape), wired `state.debug_find_local` to delegate to it, and updated `check_close_mth` and the two `get_local`/`set_local` callers to consume the owned `Vec<u8>`.

**New state:** Minimal repro now prints `variable 'x' got a non-closable value`, matching the test's expected pattern. Did not re-run the full `locals.lua` (mega-loop will re-test) — the next assert at line 511 (the `__close = nil` case) depends on a different code path (call-of-nil error path in `try_func_tm`), which should now also benefit since `funcname_from_call` was already wired correctly.
